text: The laparoscopic view displays near-infrared fluorescence imaging used to confirm the perfusion adequacy of the ileocolic anastomosis during a right hemicolectomy.
The surgeon displays the side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis under standard white light, showing the connection between the ileum and colon.
The surgeon administers five milliliters of indocyanine green via a peripheral cannula, observing the fluorescence uptake in the surgical field.
The anastomosis and the rest of the small bowel are visualized, with the small bowel serving as a positive control for fluorescence uptake.
The surgeon uses near-infrared laparoscopic fluorescence to confirm satisfactory perfusion of the ileocolic anastomosis in situ, marking the conclusion of the laparoscopic right hemicolectomy.
